title: 7 File and Report Exchange Folder Structure
body_text:
7	File and Report Exchange Folder Structure
7.1	Overview of the File and Report Exchange Folder Structure
When you log in to Central 1âs File and Report Exchange, youâll see the folders to which you have authorized access. The folder structure is the same regardless of how users access the File and Report Exchange.
7.2	File and Report Exchange Folder Structure
Users can only see the folders they are authorized to access. Some files are located in the root directory (main folder) rather than in Level 1, 2, or 3 subfolders. Since the table below contains first, second, and third level folders only, individual files may not be listed. Folders only contain content if your financial institution or business member participates in the associated product or service.
Note:	The Report Default folder is only used if a report is generated that isnât associated with another folder.
Text versions of reports are available within several consolidated report files. These are delivered to your financial institutionâs root directory on the File and Report Exchange and are in plain text format. These files have the same data as the PDF reports, but multiple reports are combined into one file. The text format may allow your financial institution to facilitate automated distribution and archiving of the reports. Within each consolidated report file, each report is identified with a four-character report code, which starts at position 20 in the report header.
The file names for reports in text format are:
MERGmmdd.DAT (AFT and Clearing reports)
ONDLmmdd.DAT (ON CAS daily reports)
DAILmmdd (BC CAS reports)
ONBLmmdd.DAT (Bill payment reports)
OMNMmmdd.DAT (CAS month-end reports)
The three folder levels are indented as follows:
